closed_loop_design.step_1: Split 2023 data by date so future days do not leak into training.
closed_loop_design.step_2: Build a truck-stop correlation graph from stop frequencies and truck purpose, then cluster trucks by behavioral similarity.
closed_loop_design.step_3: Cluster each truck's training route images and weight route patterns by that truck's own pattern frequency.
closed_loop_design.step_4: Give behavioral cluster summaries and route-pattern priors to the LLM, choose one route cluster per truck, and materialize the scenario from those chosen clusters.
closed_loop_design.step_5: Use each chosen route cluster's expected distance and stopping-zone assignment as the generated scenario.
closed_loop_design.step_6: Validate the full multi-truck scenario against held-out real per-truck and fleet-total distances.
closed_loop_design.step_7: Learn feature weights from feedback when feedback labels are available, then score validation and test periods.
closed_loop_design.step_8: Append new feedback and rerun to correct truck correlation over time.

feature_meaning.pass_score: Pearson pass correlation rescaled from [-1, 1] to [0, 1].
feature_meaning.image_similarity: Average same-date cosine similarity of trajectory image features.
feature_meaning.distance_similarity: Similarity of same-date real daily distances, combining total distance match, daily distance match, and distance-pattern correlation.
feature_meaning.purpose_match: 1 when truck purpose matches, 0 when it differs; omitted until metadata is provided.
feature_meaning.fleet_feature_similarity: Optional similarity from fleet metadata: asset type, age, kilometers, and duty cycle.

performance_goal: Closed-loop performance is evaluated by how well generated scenario route distances resemble real held-out route behavior for the full set of trucks. Pairwise distance similarity and train stability are retained as secondary diagnostics.

method.edge_weight: w_is = alpha * normalized_stop_frequency; duration is omitted.
method.truck_similarity: rho_ij = lambda * sim(stop_profile_i, stop_profile_j) + (1-lambda) * sim(purpose_i, purpose_j).

dominant_generated_scenario.definition: The generated scenario is materialized from the chosen truck-specific route cluster. Each truck receives a route-cluster assignment, expected route distance, and stopping-zone assignment from its historical stop profile.

scenario_variables.expected_route_distance_km: Expected daily route distance implied by the chosen cluster.
scenario_variables.assigned_stopping_zone: Most frequent historical stopping zone for the truck.

scenario_realism_validation.goal: Validate the generated multi-truck scenario against real held-out route distances, using each truck's training route-pattern priors as the generated expected route distance.
scenario_realism_validation.formula: 0.70 * mean per-truck relative distance error + 0.30 * fleet-total relative distance error
scenario_realism_validation.route_pattern_generation: Each truck's route clusters are weighted by that truck's own training frequency prior.

llm_weight_update.rationale: Primary evidence is scenario_realism_validation. The prior-weighted scenario generation achieves validation error 0.088 and test error 0.296, while the dominant-cluster method scores 0.412 / 0.723 — confirming that distance-based cluster selection is the strongest driver of realism. The validation-feedback (closest-to-recent-distance) method achieves 0.089 / 0.163, further reinforcing that distance_similarity is the most actionable signal. Vehicle 2897 (Fixed_plant, low-km, short-route) drives the largest per-truck errors when a wrong cluster is chosen; purpose_match is a reliable discriminator separating it from the two Mobile_Eq trucks (rho 0.566 vs 0.883), so purpose_match weight is raised slightly to help the model respect this truck-type boundary. image_similarity is present for all rows but shows weak test-period movement (drops from ~0.53 to ~0.08–0.51 across pairs), so its weight is trimmed modestly to avoid over-fitting to trajectory visuals that diverge out-of-sample. pass_score is available and stable but adds less marginal value once distance and purpose are well-weighted; it is reduced slightly. fleet_feature_similarity is fully available but all three trucks share asset_type LIGHT VEHICLE and duty_cycle MEDIUM, leaving only age/kms as discriminators — modest contribution, weight held low.
llm_weight_update.expected_effect: Increasing distance_similarity weight should anchor cluster selection more tightly to routes whose daily distance profiles match held-out behaviour, reducing per-truck relative distance error (primary metric). The small increase to purpose_match reinforces the Mobile_Eq vs Fixed_plant separation for vehicle 2897, mitigating its disproportionate error contribution. The trim to image_similarity reduces risk of selecting visually similar but distance-mismatched clusters in the test period.
risks: Vehicle 2897 test-period real distances (~9 km/day) are far below its dominant training cluster (~33 km/day); no weight adjustment fully corrects for a structural regime shift — distance_similarity alone cannot close this gap if training clusters do not contain a matching low-distance pattern.
Raising purpose_match slightly increases sensitivity to purpose metadata availability; if purpose labels are missing or noisy in future runs, this weight amplifies noise.
Trimming image_similarity may reduce discriminability for trucks whose route geometry changes without proportional distance changes, which is not observable in current data.

Fleet_feature_similarity kept low because age/kms vary substantially across the three trucks (age 2–12 years, kms 14k–231k) but all share asset type and duty cycle, so the feature adds limited pairwise contrast and could mislead if the similarity formula is dominated by the matched categorical components.

155.rationale: Validation feedback shows truck 155 averaged 46.42 km in Nov 2023, closest to cluster 1 (44.89 km, gap 1.53 km, relative gap 3.3%). Cluster 1 is the best distance match, overriding the training prior's cluster 4 selection. Cluster 1 also has reasonable prior probability (0.106) and 32 training days of support.

2897.rationale: Validation feedback shows truck 2897 averaged 15.52 km in Nov 2023, well above its dominant training cluster 1 (32.87 km) and cluster 3 (4.03 km). Cluster 7 (11.54 km) is the closest match to the validation period distance (gap 3.98 km, relative gap 25.6%), indicating a shift toward moderate-distance patterns not captured by the high-frequency training prior.

13643.rationale: Validation feedback shows truck 13643 averaged 32.74 km in Nov 2023, closest to cluster 1 (33.70 km, gap 0.96 km, relative gap 2.9%) — the tightest distance match of any cluster. Cluster 1 has reasonable prior support (0.089, 27 training days), overriding the training-selected cluster 4 (40.19 km) which is a poorer distance match for the recent period.

predicted_pairwise_correlations.rationale: Both trucks converge on cluster 1 for the test period and share Mobile_Eq purpose (purpose_match=1.0). Truck similarity rho=0.883 and learned_similarity=0.672 are high, with distance_similarity=0.706. However, their absolute distance levels differ (155~44.9 km vs 13643~33.7 km in chosen clusters), and pass_correlation is low (0.075), limiting the operational correlation to moderate.

Trucks 2897 (cluster 7, ~11.5 km) and 13643 (cluster 1, ~33.7 km) operate at very different distance scales and have different purposes (Fixed_plant vs Mobile_Eq, purpose_match=0.0). Learned_similarity=0.367 and image_similarity=0.158 are low. Scenario label is mostly_independent. Some zone-2 pass overlap partially binds them, yielding weak correlation.

Trucks 155 (cluster 1, ~44.9 km) and 2897 (cluster 7, ~11.5 km) have very different distance profiles and different purposes (Mobile_Eq vs Fixed_plant, purpose_match=0.0). Learned_similarity=0.340, image_similarity=0.172, and distance_similarity=0.403 are all low. Scenario label is mostly_independent. Minimal shared route behavior expected.

llm_scenario_generation.scenario_rationale: Validation feedback for November 2023 is the primary revision signal. Truck 155 shifted toward higher-distance clusters (cluster 1 best fits 46.4 km actual vs training-selected cluster 4 at 35.7 km). Truck 2897 moved to intermediate distances (cluster 7 at 11.5 km best fits 15.5 km actual, well above its dominant cluster 3 at 4.0 km). Truck 13643 shifted to lower distances (cluster 1 at 33.7 km is the tightest fit to 32.7 km actual vs training cluster 4 at 40.2 km). The fleet remains largely independent in the test scenario: 155 and 13643 share a cluster number and purpose, yielding moderate correlation, while 2897 operates mostly independently given its distinct purpose, scale, and route pattern.
risks: Truck 2897's cluster 7 has low prior support (4.6%, 14 training days), making the choice uncertain despite validation distance evidence.
Cluster 1 is chosen for both 155 and 13643, but their absolute distance scales (~44.9 km vs ~33.7 km) differ, so same-cluster label may overstate similarity.
No recent_behavior_context is provided; the test scenario relies entirely on validation feedback distance matching, which may not fully capture route-pattern changes.
All three trucks are in singleton behavioral clusters, limiting cross-truck cluster correlation inference from training data.
